what_it_does: A reading-order + doc-ownership index for the claude-design build-prep directory. It names the three primary docs to read (BACKEND-SEAM-PACK, the cognition authoring handoff/brief pair, APPLICATION-STRUCTURE-PACK), states which doc owns what (and does NOT own), declares BACKEND-SEAM-PACK as the one-source for the backend contract, splits the docs by session type (cognition vs interface), points at the research/ evidence corpus, and lists two studio patch files as studio-reconcile material.
observations: Defines a canonical reading order of three doc groups, with the seam pack as step 1 ('start here').
Contains an explicit ownership table (Doc | Owns | Does NOT own) for four docs.
States a 'one-source note': if the same content appears in both seam pack and structure pack, the seam pack is source of truth.
Splits work by session type: cognition session (backend+integration) vs interface session (FE structure+design).
Catalogs the research/ folder: deep/ (seven distillations named), inventory/ (inv-A through inv-E), plus INVENTORY-MAP, RESEARCH-METHOD, scout-studio-extract.
Lists two studio patches as the reconcile material: studio-fe-wiring.patch and studio-suite-mockup-focus.patch.
Lists seven deep-read filenames including fe-structure.md, contracts-governance.md, design-substrate.md, angles-delta.md (this batch's siblings).
standouts: The one-source rule naming BACKEND-SEAM-PACK.md as authoritative over the structure pack for the backend contract.
The division-by-session-type mapping that assigns specific doc sets to each kind of build session.
conventions: Follows a 'one doc owns each concern, others cross-reference' documentation discipline.
Uses an explicit owns/does-not-own table rather than prose to fix boundaries.
Frames the research/ corpus as a 'bounded-read' evidence base that produced the packs.
notes: This is the table-of-contents / navigation note for the claude-design pack — read it to know which of the sibling docs to open and which is authoritative.
questions: The deep/ list names seven files (incl. surface-intent.md, seams.md, selfdesc-ops.md) but only four deep-reads are in this batch — are the others elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it states it is a reading-order + doc-ownership index and that is exactly its content.
apparent_themes: Separation of concerns by document ownership
Evidence-grounded design (a bounded-read corpus behind the packs)
intention_signals: Trying to prevent doc drift and duplication across a multi-doc design pack by fixing one authoritative source per concern and routing readers to the right doc for their session type.
novelty: The 'division by session type' that hands each kind of build session a tailored doc set, plus an explicit does-NOT-own column.
contribution: Contributes the navigation map and ownership boundaries for the claude-design doc set — the index that tells a reader which pack is authoritative for the backend contract and which docs belong to which session type.
summary_for_embedding: Reading-order and doc-ownership index for the claude-design build-prep pack, naming BACKEND-SEAM-PACK as the one-source backend contract and splitting docs by cognition vs interface session.
intention_for_embedding: To orient a builder to the claude-design design docs, fix one authoritative source per concern, and route each session type to its correct doc set.
